name: Defense & Aerospace Sector
summary: Day 60 Monday — BRANCH 3 RESOLUTION REMOVES DEFENSE-ROTATION RE-ACTIVATION TAIL; LMT Q1 MISS = ~0% CRISIS ATTRIBUTION; STRUCTURAL ENTRY ZONE PRESERVED. Friday close: LMT $513.45 (-3.1% 1D, -17.7% 1M, -11.3% 3M) on Q1 EARNINGS MISS — EPS $6.44 vs consensus $6.74; revenue $18.0B vs $18.2B; NET MARGIN 6.4% vs 7.7% YoY; FCF NEGATIVE -$291M; F-35 program charges. ITA $215.80 (-1.5%, -4.5% 1M, -7.5% 3M); URA $55.31 (-2.1% 1D, +13.0% 1M). Cumulative LMT two-session drawdown ~-7.4% from Wednesday's $571.95. Susquehanna $700 PT held with margin compression flagged. BRANCH 3 RESOLUTION (Brent gap -4.6% to $100.47 on Sunday Globex / Monday Asia) confirms Branch 3 over Branch 1 (structural break) and Branch 2 (theatrical) — DEFENSE-ROTATION RE-ACTIVATION TAIL REMOVED. The Sunday-news two-mechanism exposure (Vahidi-targeting CNN leak + Pentagon Hormuz strike plans) tested as positioning unwind, not crisis-beta firing. Yesterday's brief flagged 'investor entry-zone window for LMT $510-515 is ASYMMETRICALLY at risk on Branch 1; Branch 2/3 preserves' — Branch 3 PRESERVES the entry zone STRUCTURALLY. LMT at $510-515 IS THE 90-DAY CLEAN ENTRY WINDOW on structural thesis ($1.5T FY27 budget, NATO 5% GDP target, missile-production ramps, THAAD/PAC-3/PrSM structural demand). Friday's LMT -3.1% is fundamentals (program execution + working capital), not crisis-flow. ITA -1.5% likely tests $208 area on Branch 3 deepening (sector-broader margin concern spreading from LMT). Macro defense thesis intact at structural level; cyclical war-bid component unwinding completes through Branch 3 architecture.
